options.content:
**How to read this section:** Monitors the physical health of your Python engine, SQLite database, and Zeek collectors.
* 🟢 **Normal:** Collector lag < 2 seconds. Zeek Status is 1. The Script Memory Footprint is a flat, horizontal line. Alert Queue stays at 0.
* 🔴 **Abnormal / Threat:** A diagonal memory line indicates a leak. Lag > 15s means the Pi-hole database is locked. Anti-Poisoning Freezes > 0 means the script detected a Day-Zero threat and intentionally locked its own learning memory to prevent baseline poisoning.

spec.title: Threat Thresholds vs Safe Baseline Corridor (Query Rate)

options.content:
**How to read this section:** Your 30,000-foot daily driver. It provides a macro view of every device and immediately highlights rule-breakers.
* 🟢 **Normal:** The 'Severe Threats' counter is 0. On the corridor chart, the solid live traffic lines stay comfortably beneath the dotted 'Threat Limit' ceiling.
* 🔴 **Abnormal / Threat:** Solid traffic lines breaking violently through the top of the dotted Threat Limit line. Any device glowing red (Score > 6.0) in the Threat Ledger requires immediate investigation below.

spec.title: Geo Threat Map (Size = Volume, Color = Threat Level)

options.content:
**How to leverage this terminal:** This is your active forensic workstation fed directly via Promtail from your `alerts_stream.jsonl` pipeline.
* When Prometheus components spike above, click their contextual data link to automatically target queries into this log container.
* Use the metadata schema inside the viewer to analyze localized execution paths, factor scores, and exact cryptographic IOC details instantly.

spec.title: Live Master Alert Payload Stream (Loki Integration)

options.content:
**How to read this section:** Skips behavioral guesswork and looks for absolute, verified malware signatures using integrated feeds (AbuseIPDB, URLhaus, VirusTotal) and Zeek TLS fingerprints.
* 🟢 **Normal:** These panels should be completely flat at zero.
* 🔴 **Abnormal / Threat:** JA3 Hits > 0 means a device is initiating an encrypted connection using the exact TLS handshake of known malware (Cobalt Strike, Trickbot). TI Risk Spikes mean a device successfully connected to an explicitly listed global hacker blocklist.

spec.expr: {filename=~".*_stream.jsonl"} | json | hostname=~"$device" |~ "(?i)threat intelligence ioc|abuseipdb blacklist|virustotal detection|malicious ja3"

options.content:
**How to read this section:** Domain Generation Algorithms (DGAs) are used by ransomware and botnets to randomly guess thousands of gibberish domains (e.g., `xkqztp.ru`) to find a live command server.
* 🟢 **Normal:** NXDOMAIN (failed lookup) ratios hover below 10%. New domains occasionally bump up when visiting new websites.
* 🔴 **Abnormal / Threat:** A massive, simultaneous vertical spike in 'New Domains' and the 'NXDOMAIN Ratio'. If NXDOMAIN TLD Concentration hits > 80%, the malware is hyper-focusing its failed guesses on a specific Top Level Domain (like `.ru` or `.cc`).

spec.expr: {filename=~".*_stream.jsonl"} | json | hostname=~"$device" |~ "(?i)nxdomain ratio|blocked dns ratio|suspicious.*dga|first-seen domain burst|nxdomain tld concentration|probationary|correlated dns baseline"

options.content:
**How to read this section:** Malware 'beacons' or 'calls home' to a hacker's server periodically. Advanced malware does this slowly (e.g., exactly once every 5 minutes) to evade volume-based detection.
* 🟢 **Normal:** Human web browsing is erratic and random. The Jitter chart should be completely empty.
* 🔴 **Abnormal / Threat:** A bar appearing here means Zeek/Pi-hole tracked requests to a single destination with mechanical, robotic timing precision (a Coefficient of Variation under 10%). This is a confirmed, active Command & Control channel.

spec.title: Single Domain Concentration Intensity (Top Domain Ratio)
Minimum Timing Coefficient of Variation (Jitter CV Base)
🔎 Filtered Workspace: C2 Periodic Beaconing Analysis
Anti-Poisoning Freezes
spec.description: Devices currently frozen out of baseline learning this cycle (risk>=7.0, TI match, malicious JA3, DoH bypass, or lateral movement). Backed by home_ids_baseline_poisoned.

options.content:
**How to read this section:** Attackers steal data either by sneaking it out through long DNS subdomains (e.g., `stolen-password-string.hacker.com`) or by blasting a massive TCP/UDP upload.
* 🟢 **Normal:** Deep Domains stay near zero (standard websites rarely use >4 sub-labels). The Outbound Bytes Z-Score casually fluctuates between -2 and +2.
* 🔴 **Abnormal / Threat:** Continuous high counts of Deep Domains mean data is being actively exfiltrated via DNS tunneling. An Exfiltration Z-Score > 5.0 means a massive payload was uploaded, completely shattering historical baselines.

spec.expr: {filename=~".*_stream.jsonl"} | json | hostname=~"$device" |~ "(?i)exfiltration payload burst|deep dns structures|deep subdomains"

options.content:
**How to read this section:** Catches devices trying to bypass your Pi-hole entirely, or compromised internal devices turning around and attacking other devices on your home network.
* 🟢 **Normal:** All panels are completely flat at zero.
* 🔴 **Abnormal / Threat:** DoH Bypass > 0 means a device (often a smart TV or malware) is hardcoded to use Cloudflare/Google DNS directly via HTTPS, blinding your Pi-hole. Lateral Movement > 0 is critical: an internal device is actively port-scanning or attempting SSH/SMB connections against another private device (worm spreading).

spec.title: 🔎 Filtered Workspace: Defense Evasion & Lateral Movements

spec.expr: {filename=~".*_stream.jsonl"} | json | hostname=~"$device" |~ "(?i)doh tunneling bypass|internal lateral movement|suspicious destination port|zeek notice"

options.content:
**How to read this section:** The IsolationForest AI engine finds stealthy, 'living-off-the-land' attacks that keep their volume low enough to bypass human-made thresholds.
* 🟢 **Normal:** ML Score stays very close to 0.00.
* 🔴 **Abnormal / Threat:** ML Score > 0.05 means the AI detected a structural matrix shift (the pattern of traffic is fundamentally alien to that device's history). High Risk Velocity indicates the risk score shot up violently compared to its own rolling hourly average.

spec.title: Internal Lateral Movement (SSH, RDP, SMB scanning)
TCP/UDP Data Exfiltration Z-Score (Outbound Zeek Bytes)

options.content:
**How to trace routing parameters:** Correlates external network endpoints back to their upstream routing providers (ASNs).
* Ensure `geoip_asn_db` is initialized inside `config.json` to avoid single unknown bucket limits.
* Target malicious ASNs returning blocklist flags directly inside threat maps below.

spec.title: Risk Magnitude Allocation by ASN Matrix
Top External Target Routing Providers (ASN Volume)
Threat Feed Hit Counters mapped by ASN Infrastructure
Distinct Internal Device Footprints via ASN targets
Unique Target Domain Cardinality tracked per ASN

options.content:
**How to read this section:** Visual mapping of where your network packets are physically landing. (Bubble Size = Traffic Volume; Bubble Color = Threat Level).
* 🟢 **Normal:** Large green bubbles over your home country, the US, and standard infrastructure hubs (AWS, Azure, Google).
* 🔴 **Abnormal / Threat:** Red bubbles over high-risk jurisdictions. The 'C2 Beaconing Maps' panel lighting up on a specific foreign ASN, showing exactly where a compromised device is sending its check-ins.

**How to read this section:** Shows what the IPS auto-mitigation engine (ips.py) has actually *done* — not just detected. Only fires when risk ≥ 8.0, a live C2 beacon, or a confirmed DGA burst is seen.
* 🟢 **Normal:** IPS Enabled shows your configured state; Blocks/Isolations/Errors stay at 0 most of the time.
* 🔴 **Abnormal:** Any Isolation, or a rising Errors counter (Pi-hole/router webhook auth or connectivity problem — mitigations are silently failing).

spec.title: 🔍 Interactive Incident Response & Loki Triage Room
